Juxtaposition involves placing two contrasting elements together to provoke thought and is a fundamental element in comics.
Juxtaposition is a technique used in comics to place two contrasting elements together to provoke thought or evoke humor, requiring readers to pause and reassess the meaning in a nonlinear way.

The work examines VLMs' ability to understand comics, focusing on humor derived from juxtaposition, and aims to determine if large models can comprehend the complex and contradictory narratives present in comics.
Contradictions in comics challenge conventional semantic interpretations, requiring deeper analysis to understand the meaning behind juxtaposition.

node_2: recognition and interpretation of visual humor
edge: The proposed benchmark focuses on recognizing and interpreting visual humor through juxtaposition.
AI is being explored for not only interpreting but also creatively engaging with content.
All data samples are collected from publicly available content on social media platforms.
Copyright compliance is ensured by using original links to comics without infringement.
Permission is obtained from the author artist, such as Anton Gudim, for using sourced content.
The author artist such as Anton Gudim and Liz Climo gave us permission to use their public images for our benchmark.
We committed to open-sourcing our annotated benchmark, providing corresponding links to each comic image.
The Large Vision Language Models utilized in our experiments are pretrained using diverse web corpora, which may introduce biases in their outputs.
We advise users to conscientiously evaluate the ethical implications of generated outputs when employing them in future research endeavors.
Eight human judges are engaged in our annotation process and are compensated with an average hourly wage of $11 for their contributions.
